Peak Perks
Peak Perks
Got a smart thermostat? Get paid to reduce your energy use and help out Ontario's grid.
When you enroll:
$75 virtual prepaid Mastercard ®
Each year you stay enrolled:
$20 virtual prepaid Mastercard ®
Enroll today
Canada’s Biggest Virtual Power Plant: 300,000 Strong and Growing!
The impact is undeniable—over 300,000 participants and growing every day ! Canada’s biggest virtual power plant is revolutionizing how Ontario powers up. Together, we’ve saved enough energy to supply an entire city the size of Oshawa —and we’re just getting started.
What is Peak Perks?​
The fastest-growing demand response program in North America.
Participants allow brief, time-limited smart thermostat adjustments during periods of peak electricity demand on the hottest summer days.
Great for you
Get $75 when you sign up and $20 each year you stay enrolled
Reduce your energy use at home or at work
Great for your neighbours
Help reduce demand on Ontario’s grid and support reliable, affordable energy for all
Don't have a smart thermostat yet?
We have a rebate for that!
How it works
Sign up for Peak Perks with an eligible smart thermostat. Here’s what to expect as a participant this summer:
Before an adjustment
Your thermostat may be adjusted down slightly to pre-cool your home or business so that you stay comfortable.
During an adjustment
Your thermostat will be automatically adjusted no more than two degrees Celsius, for up to three hours.
After an adjustment
Your thermostat will automatically return to its original temperature setting.
You’re always in control
You’ll be notified before every adjustment and can opt out at any time. To opt out, adjust your thermostat’s temperature either manually or on your provider’s mobile app.
Eligibility
To participate, you must:
Be a residential electricity customer in Ontario* with central air conditioning that is controlled by your smart thermostat. Heat pumps that are part of central air conditioning are also eligible.
Be a business with 50 or fewer employees on site at the business at any point in time, under the full operating capacity of that business.
Have an eligible Wi-Fi enabled smart thermostat installed in your home or business.
Not be participating in another demand response program in Ontario where your thermostat is automatically adjusted to help maintain reliability of the electricity grid. Please note: Residents of Sarnia who also participate in the Enbridge Gas Sarnia Saves demand response program can also participate in Peak Perks.

Energy Affordability Program
The Energy Affordability Program offers energy-saving upgrades tailored to the specific needs of your home, all at no cost. And a more energy-efficient home means more comfort and more money in your wallet. Saving energy is simple with the Energy Affordability Program.
Apply now

What is the Energy Affordability Program?
The Energy Affordability Program provides support to income-eligible electricity consumers by helping them to better manage their monthly electricity costs and to increase their home comfort.
There are two types of support available through the Energy Affordability Program: Comprehensive Support and Energy Saving Kits .
All expert advice and energy-saving home upgrades under this program are completely free of charge.
The Energy Affordability Program is delivered by trusted Save on Energy delivery agents .
Save on Energy is expanding eligibility for cold climate air source heat pumps within the Energy Affordability Program (EAP), thanks to funding support from Natural Resources Canada (NRCan) through its Oil to Heat Pump Affordability (OHPA) program.
As a result, eligible homeowners with oil-fueled heating systems may now qualify to receive a high-efficiency cold climate heat pump system at no cost . These systems can help participants reduce energy costs, improve home comfort, increase property value, and lower their carbon footprint.
The EAP is a fully funded home energy retrofit program designed for income-eligible Ontarians.
Apply now to the Energy Affordability Program
Comprehensive support
Ontario residents who qualify for this level of support will have an energy-efficiency expert visit their home to conduct a free energy needs assessment to identify opportunities to help them save energy, receive upgrades and replace eligible appliances.
What's Included
Depending on eligibility and the existing equipment within your home, you may receive:
LED light bulbs
Energy-efficient refrigerators
Energy-efficient freezers
Energy-efficient dehumidifiers
Energy-efficient window air conditioner
Drying rack for clothes
Smart power strip
High-efficiency showerheads (standard and handheld)*
Faucet aerators (kitchen and bathroom)*
Additional attic or basement insulation**
Draft proofing**
Programmable or smart thermostat(s)**
Cold climate air source heat pump **
*for homes with electric hot water heating
**for homes heated by electricity or oil
Eligibility
To qualify, the participant must be:
A resident of an eligible Social Housing property, OR
An individual who owns, rents or leases a residence, is listed as the primary or secondary utility account holder, AND meets ONE of the following criteria:

Program News
May 29, 2026
Retrofit Program Updates
Save on Energy programs are designed to be cost-effective for Ontario ratepayers. We therefore need to adjust program incentives from time to time to reflect changes in market conditions, program participation, and to align program spending with our energy and peak demand savings objectives. The spring 2026 Retrofit program changes improve program affordability, cost-effectiveness and ensure that we support program participants as well as deliver a net benefit to ratepayers.
Two sets of changes are happening this spring.
Changes effective May 29, 2026:
Prescriptive Packaged Terminal Heat Pump (PTHP) are now split into two measure tiers (one for replacements and one for new installations):
Prescriptive PTHP (New Installations): only available for hotel and motel suites based on expected baseline (same as current requirements)
Prescriptive PTHP (Replacements): for replacements of existing packaged terminal air conditioners and electric resistance heating
Removed eligibility for wall-mounted Solar DERs (Distributed Energy Resources)
Updated Solar DER worksheet to improve usability and improve accuracy for savings assumptions by introducing updated tilt angle entry field (applicants now need to enter tilt angle instead of selecting closest applicable tilt angle from dropdown)
In addition to the changes effective May 29, 2026 , the following changes come into effect on June 30, 2026 .
Changes effective June 30, 2026:
Adjusted incentive by 10% for Small-to-Medium Generation Solar DER (>10kW) from $860/kW to $770/kW-AC nameplate capacity
Adjusted incentive by 10% for LED Grow Lights for Vegetables ($200/fixture to $180/fixture at $0.15/kWh to $0.13/kWh) and Cannabis Warehouses ($200/fixture to $180/fixture)
Adjusted incentive by 15% for Horticultural Lighting Controls and Network Lighting Controls ($0.35/kWh to $0.30/kWh)
Introducing new Prescriptive Clean Water Pump (CWP) measure which provides incentive of $15/HP for qualifying energy-efficient pumps
More details on these changes are available here .
Applications containing measures that will have their incentives reduced on June 30, 2026, and that are submitted before June 30, 2026, will be honoured based on the incentives at the time of application submission. Applicants are expected to submit a complete application.
These changes to the Retrofit program are part of a series of planned, semi-annual updates that will be made to the program on an ongoing basis. The next program update is expected to be made in Fall 2026.
If you have any questions, please reach out to saveonenergy@ieso.ca for further information.
Program News
April 17, 2026
Launch of new Peak Performance Program
This new program pays commercial and institutional buildings to reduce their HVAC use on peak demand days in the summer. Participants are notified in advance of events, choose how much to curtail each time, and get paid based on verified energy savings— with no penalties for non‑participation. New participants may also qualify for an additional enabling incentive to help offset setup costs. Learn more here.
Program News
January 1, 2026
Energy Performance Program (EPP) doubles the incentives and introduces a new program portal
With the new year comes new incentives and a new program portal for the Energy Performance Program! Participants who enrolled as of January 1, 2025 now save $0.30/kWh during summer peak hours and $0.08/kWh for non-summer peak hours. The new EPP portal has been enhanced to improve the application and login process, as well as a number of other features. Learn more here .
